turns.content: Why does this even work this way? I'm trying to pipe output from a child process into another command, but it's not behaving as expected. Can you explain how std::process::Command handles stdin and stdout?

Absolutely! Let me break down what happens here. When you call `std::process::Command`, you're essentially telling the Rust runtime to spawn a new process, similar to running a command from your shell. One thing to note is how stdin and stdout are handled: by default, child processes inherit stdin/stdout of the parent (usually terminal), but you can override this behavior.
So if I want to pipe output from one command into another, do I have to manually set up pipes?

Yes! Rust's `Command` API gives you control over stdin/stdout via the `stdout()` and `stdin()` methods. Here’s a mental model: imagine each process as a water pipe; by default, the parent pumps data into child.stdin and reads from child.stdout. If you want to connect two processes (say, command A's stdout -> command B's stdin), you’ll need a manual setup — essentially creating an inter-process pipe.
